topic: grandchild doing well in school — proud grandfather

turns.content: friend I have to tell somebody. my granddaughter get top mark again. number one in whole grade. math and science both, you know. I am so happy today
That's wonderful news! Top of the entire grade in both math and science is seriously impressive. You have every reason to be proud. How old is she?
she 11 year old. small girl but brain very sharp, you know. teacher call her parents, say she is exceptional student. not just good. exceptional. I tell everybody at market this morning

Ha! I bet everyone at the market heard about it. 'Exceptional' from a teacher carries real weight — they see hundreds of students and don't use that word lightly. Does she enjoy the studying, or is she one of those kids who just picks it up naturally?

both I think. she come to my house weekend, bring book, sit at table and study while I fix things around house. I say go play outside, she say no ong noi I have homework. very serious face, you know. hah. 11 year old more discipline than some adult

Calling you ong noi with that serious homework face — that's a beautiful picture. The discipline at 11 is remarkable. Most kids that age want to run around and play, not sit at a table with books. She clearly gets her work ethic from the family.

yes. she bring report card to show me herself. run in the door, ong noi look look! very excited. score 9.5, 9.8, all high number. I put on wall in living room so everybody who visit can see. one whole week it stay on wall

Her running in to show you first — that's the best part. She's not just studying for grades, she wants to make her grandfather proud. And putting the report card on the wall tells her that the whole family values her hard work. 9.5 and 9.8 are outstanding. Does she know what she wants to be when she grows up?

she want to be doctor. since she small she say that. I think she see people in village get sick, no good doctor nearby, you know. nearest hospital far away. she say she want come back and help people here. good heart

A doctor who comes back to help her own community — that's a special ambition for an 11-year-old. Most kids that age just say doctor because it sounds impressive, but she already has a reason behind it. Medical school is very competitive, but a student already top of the grade at 11 is building the right foundation.

medical school very expensive though, you know. I think about that sometime. but long way away still. right now just keep her happy, keep her studying. education is like shrimp pond, friend. you put in good water, good feed, take care every day — she grow strong. cannot rush it

That's a perfect way to think about it — good water, good feed, patience every day. And just like a pond, if you try to rush or cut corners, you end up with problems. She's got years before medical school, and a lot can happen. Scholarships exist for exceptional students. One step at a time.

The fact that she has a grandfather who wakes before dawn every day and works hard — that's the best example she could have. She's learning discipline by watching you.
hah. she say to me one time, ong noi you wake up too early. I say if I not wake up early, shrimp not happy. she laugh. but she understand, you know. hard work make good result. she see that every day

Ha! If the shrimp aren't happy, nobody's happy. She's learning the connection between effort and reward just by growing up around you. That's something no textbook can teach.

A good harvest feeds the family for a season. A good education feeds the family for a lifetime. Keep putting those report cards on the wall.
yes. okay friend I am going now. just want share good news with somebody today, you know. make old man heart feel light. sometime life hard but day like this make everything worth it. bye bye

Thank you for sharing it — days like this are what all the hard work is for. Tell your granddaughter to keep at it, and tell her that ong noi's wall is waiting for the next report card. Have a good day!
